title: Trajectories of Paternal Contact After Parental Separation: A Latent Class Growth Analysis
abstract: A latent class growth curve analysis was conducted to model post-separation, paternal involvement trajectories over an 8-year period from the time of separation. Our sample was composed of 245 separated mothers whose data was drawn from a representative survey of Quebec children. Four classes of distinct trajectories were identified: shared-time, near-shared-time, moderate-stable, and low-decreasing. The majority of fathers maintained a consistently high level of contact with their children, and the frequency of visits at the time of separation seemed to determine future father-child contact. Several factors predicted contact trajectories, including socioeconomic status, a new partner in the mother's life, and the quality of the inter-parental relationship.
authors_text: Baude, Amandine; Drapeau, Sylvie; Lachance, Veronique; Ivers, Hans
